11.label: Down the Rabbit-Hole
11.recap: Bored by the riverbank, Alice follows a waistcoated White Rabbit down a hole and lands in a hall of locked doors.

11.label: The Pool of Tears
11.recap: After shooting up tall and shrinking small, Alice weeps a whole pool of tears and swims off through it.

11.label: A Caucus-Race and a Long Tale
11.recap: A soggy crowd of birds and animals dries off with a muddled Caucus-Race, then drifts away one by one.

11.label: The Rabbit Sends in a Little Bill
11.recap: Mistaken for the Rabbit's maid, Alice grows enormous inside his house before nibbling her way back out and fleeing.

11.label: Advice from a Caterpillar
11.recap: A hookah-smoking Caterpillar quizzes Alice on who she is, then leaves her a mushroom that changes her size.

11.label: Pig and Pepper
11.recap: In a pepper-filled kitchen Alice meets the Duchess, finds the grinning Cheshire Cat, and rescues a baby that turns into a pig.

11.label: A Mad Tea-Party
11.recap: The Hatter, the March Hare, and a drowsy Dormouse pull Alice into a riddling, never-ending tea-party.

11.label: The Queen's Croquet-Ground
11.recap: Gardeners frantically paint white roses red as the fierce Queen of Hearts sweeps Alice into a chaotic croquet match.

11.label: The Mock Turtle's Story
11.recap: The Duchess waxes on about morals, then a wistful Mock Turtle and a Gryphon recount their odd schooling by the sea.

11.label: The Lobster Quadrille
11.recap: The Mock Turtle and Gryphon dance the Lobster Quadrille and coax Alice through some thoroughly muddled recitations.

11.label: Who Stole the Tarts?
11.recap: At the trial of the Knave of Hearts, witnesses give nonsense testimony while Alice quietly begins to grow again.

11.label: Alice's Evidence
11.recap: Alice towers over the court, scatters the Queen's nonsense, and wakes on the riverbank to find it was all a dream.

35.label: The Machine
35.recap: At his dinner party the Time Traveller shows a small glittering machine and sends it vanishing into time before his startled guests.

35.label: Time Travelling
35.recap: Returning dishevelled a week later, the Traveller tells how he first hurtled forward through the centuries.

35.label: In the Golden Age
35.recap: He arrives in the year 802,701 among the gentle, childlike Eloi in a soft and crumbling paradise.

35.label: The Sunset of Mankind
35.recap: Puzzling over the frail Eloi, the Traveller discovers his time machine has been dragged away and hidden.

35.label: The Morlocks
35.recap: He uncovers the pale, underground Morlocks and the grim truth of how the two races of the future now live.

35.label: The Palace of Green Porcelain
35.recap: Hunting for a weapon, the Traveller and his Eloi friend Weena explore a vast ruined museum of the past.

35.label: The Trap of the White Sphinx
35.recap: Fighting the Morlocks in the dark to reach his machine, the Traveller loses Weena in the chaos.

35.label: The Further Vision
35.recap: Escaping forward in time, he travels on to a dying earth beneath a vast, cold, red sun.

35.label: The Traveller's Return
35.recap: He races home to his laboratory and his waiting guests, exhausted and forever changed by what he has seen.

43.label: Story of the Door
43.recap: Lawyer Utterson hears how a cold, brutal man named Hyde trampled a child and paid the family off with a cheque signed by the respected Dr Jekyll.

43.label: Search for Mr. Hyde
43.recap: Troubled by Jekyll's will leaving everything to Hyde, Utterson haunts the door until he finally meets the chilling man face to face.

43.label: Dr. Jekyll Was Quite at Ease
43.recap: Over dinner Jekyll airily waves off Utterson's worries, asking only that, whatever should happen, Hyde be looked after.

43.label: The Carew Murder Case
43.recap: Hyde beats a kindly old gentleman to death in the street, and all London turns out in a manhunt for him.

43.label: Incident of the Letter
43.recap: Hyde has vanished. A pale, shaken Jekyll shows Utterson a farewell letter from Hyde, but its handwriting plants a fresh doubt.

43.label: Incident of Dr. Lanyon
43.recap: With Hyde gone Jekyll grows sociable again, then suddenly shuts himself away, while old Dr Lanyon sickens under some secret dread and dies.

43.label: Incident at the Window
43.recap: Chatting up at his window, Jekyll's face twists into such horror that Utterson and Enfield flee the courtyard in silence.

43.label: The Last Night
43.recap: Jekyll's butler Poole comes in terror: a stranger's voice is locked in the cabinet, and the two men resolve to break down the door.

43.label: Dr. Lanyon's Narrative
43.recap: Lanyon's sealed letter reveals what he saw that night: a potion drunk, and a transformation so impossible it shattered him.

43.label: Henry Jekyll's Full Statement
43.recap: In his own hand, Jekyll confesses the whole truth of his double life, and how it finally consumed him.

46.label: Marley's Ghost
46.recap: On Christmas Eve the miser Scrooge sneers at the season, until his dead partner Marley's chained ghost warns that three spirits will visit him.

46.label: The First of the Spirits
46.recap: The Ghost of Christmas Past carries Scrooge through his lonely boyhood, a kind old employer, and the sweetheart he lost to greed.

46.label: The Second of the Spirits
46.recap: The Ghost of Christmas Present reveals the warm, poor Cratchit home and frail Tiny Tim, and the world's mingled joy and want.

46.label: The Last of the Spirits
46.recap: A silent, hooded phantom shows Scrooge a death no one mourns, and lets him read his own name upon a neglected grave.

46.label: The End of It
46.recap: Scrooge wakes on Christmas morning overjoyed and made new, and keeps Christmas generously for the rest of his days.

55.label: The Cyclone
55.recap: A cyclone carries Dorothy, her dog Toto, and the whole farmhouse far away from the grey Kansas prairie.

55.label: The Munchkins
55.recap: The house lands on the Wicked Witch of the East; the grateful Munchkins give Dorothy silver shoes and send her to the Wizard for help getting home.

55.label: The Scarecrow
55.recap: On the yellow brick road Dorothy frees a friendly Scarecrow who wishes only for brains.

55.label: The Tin Woodman
55.recap: They free a rusted Tin Woodman who longs above all for a heart.

55.label: The Cowardly Lion
55.recap: A great but timid Lion joins the company, hoping the Wizard can grant him courage.

55.label: The Poppy Field
55.recap: A field of deadly poppies nearly claims Dorothy and the Lion before the others carry them to safety.

55.label: The Emerald City
55.recap: In the dazzling Emerald City the Wizard appears differently to each, and will help only if they destroy the Wicked Witch of the West.

55.label: The Wicked Witch
55.recap: The Witch sets wolves, crows, and winged monkeys on the travelers, then captures Dorothy as a slave.

55.label: The Witch Melts
55.recap: Dorothy melts the Wicked Witch with a bucket of water and frees her friends and the Winged Monkeys.

55.label: The Man Behind the Curtain
55.recap: Back in the city they discover the great and terrible Oz is only an ordinary humbug hiding behind a curtain.

55.label: Wishes Granted
55.recap: The humbug Wizard cleverly gives the Scarecrow, the Woodman, and the Lion the brains, heart, and courage they craved.

55.label: Home Again
55.recap: Glinda reveals the silver shoes can carry Dorothy anywhere, and she returns at last to Kansas and Aunt Em.

74.label: Tom in Trouble
74.recap: Mischievous orphan Tom dodges his Aunt Polly, skips school for a swim, and scraps with a newcomer.

74.label: Tom Meets Becky
74.recap: Dreading school, Tom is smitten at first sight by the new girl in town, Becky Thatcher.

74.label: Murder in the Graveyard
74.recap: Sneaking to the graveyard at midnight, Tom and Huck witness Injun Joe murder the doctor and frame poor Muff Potter.

74.label: Off to be Pirates
74.recap: Heartsick over Becky, Tom runs off with Huck and Joe to live as pirates on Jackson's Island.

74.label: Their Own Funeral
74.recap: Given up for drowned, the boys creep back to town and stroll in on their own funeral, to everyone's joy.

74.label: Tom Takes the Blame
74.recap: At school Tom nobly takes Becky's whipping to spare her from disgrace.

74.label: The Trial
74.recap: At Muff Potter's murder trial Tom bravely testifies to the truth, and Injun Joe leaps from the window and escapes.

74.label: Hunting Treasure
74.recap: Tom and Huck go hunting for buried treasure and end up creeping through a haunted house.

74.label: Injun Joe's Gold
74.recap: In the haunted house the boys watch Injun Joe dig up a box of gold and carry it off to a secret hiding place.

74.label: Lost in the Cave
74.recap: Tom and Becky lose their way deep in McDougal's Cave, where Injun Joe is also hiding.

74.label: Injun Joe's End
74.recap: After Tom and Becky's rescue the cave is sealed, and Injun Joe is found dead inside it.

74.label: Rich and Respectable
74.recap: Tom and Huck recover the hidden gold, become the town's heroes, and the Widow Douglas takes Huck in.

76.label: Civilizing Huck
76.recap: Adopted by the Widow Douglas and schooled by Miss Watson, restless Huck slips out for midnight adventures with Tom Sawyer.

76.label: Pap Returns
76.recap: Huck's drunken father reappears to grab his money and shuts Huck away in a lonely riverside cabin.

76.label: The Escape
76.recap: Huck fakes his own murder to be free of Pap and slips away down the river to Jackson's Island.

76.label: Jim on the Island
76.recap: On the island Huck finds Jim, who has run off to escape being sold away from his family.

76.label: Down the River
76.recap: Disguised as a girl, Huck learns men are hunting Jim, and the two push off down the Mississippi on a raft.

76.label: Lost in the Fog
76.recap: A thick fog parts Huck and Jim on the water, and Huck's cruel trick afterward shames him into apologizing to his friend.

76.label: The Feud
76.recap: Taken in by the genteel Grangerfords, Huck is swept into their bloody feud with the Shepherdsons.

76.label: The Duke and the King
76.recap: Two con men styling themselves a duke and a king commandeer the raft and pull Huck and Jim into their swindles.

76.label: The Wilks Fraud
76.recap: The pair pose as the long-lost brothers of the dead Mr Wilks to rob his trusting orphaned daughters.

76.label: Huck's Choice
76.recap: After the con men sell Jim, Huck wrestles with his conscience and resolves to free his friend, whatever it costs his soul.

76.label: At the Phelps Farm
76.recap: Mistaken for Tom Sawyer at the farm where Jim is held, Huck is joined by the real Tom and a wild rescue scheme.

76.label: Setting Jim Free
76.recap: Tom's needlessly elaborate escape goes wrong and he is shot, but the truth that Jim is already free finally comes out.

84.label: Walton's Letters
84.recap: Explorer Robert Walton writes home from the edge of the Arctic, longing for a true friend as his ship pushes north.

84.label: A Stranger on the Ice
84.recap: Walton's crew rescues a wasted, haunted traveller from the ice, Victor Frankenstein, who begins to tell his tale.

84.label: Victor's Childhood
84.recap: Victor recalls a happy Geneva boyhood and his beloved adopted sister Elizabeth.

84.label: The Spark of Life
84.recap: At university Victor masters natural philosophy and grows obsessed with discovering the secret of life itself.

84.label: The Creature Wakes
84.recap: On a dreary November night Victor gives his creation life, then flees in horror at the thing he has made.

84.label: News of a Death
84.recap: A letter from home brings terrible news: Victor's young brother William has been murdered.

84.label: Justine's Trial
84.recap: The gentle servant Justine is tried and condemned for William's death, though Victor dreads who is truly to blame.

84.label: Meeting on the Glacier
84.recap: High on a mountain glacier the creature finds Victor and demands, at last, to be heard.

84.label: The Creature's Tale
84.recap: The creature recounts its lonely awakening and how, unseen, it slowly learned to feel and to survive.

84.label: Rejected by All
84.recap: After secretly learning tenderness from a poor family, the creature is driven off in terror the moment it is seen.

84.label: A Terrible Bargain
84.recap: The creature demands that Victor build him a mate, swearing to vanish from mankind forever in return.

84.label: The Promise Broken
84.recap: Sickened by his task, Victor destroys the half-finished second creature, and his enemy vows a terrible revenge.

84.label: The Pursuit North
84.recap: All he loved now taken from him, Victor chases the creature across the frozen north, back to where Walton found him.

174.label: The Portrait
174.recap: Painter Basil completes a portrait of the beautiful young Dorian Gray while his friend Lord Henry preaches a creed of pleasure.

174.label: A Fatal Wish
174.recap: Intoxicated by Lord Henry's words, Dorian wishes the portrait would age in his place while he stays forever young.

174.label: Sibyl Vane
174.recap: Dorian falls headlong for a poor young actress, Sibyl Vane, dazzled by her talent on the stage.

174.label: Prince Charming
174.recap: Sibyl glows with love for the gentleman she calls her Prince Charming, as her brother James swears to protect her.

174.label: A Cruelty
174.recap: When Sibyl acts badly, a disgusted Dorian casts her off, and that night the painted face shows its first cruel change.

174.label: The Secret Hidden
174.recap: Learning Sibyl has died, Dorian hardens his heart and locks the changing portrait away out of sight.

174.label: Basil's Worry
174.recap: Basil pleads to understand Dorian's new coldness, but Dorian guards his terrible secret.

174.label: Years of Excess
174.recap: Dorian sinks into years of secret vice while his face stays unmarked and the hidden portrait grows monstrous.

174.label: Basil's Last Visit
174.recap: Basil corners Dorian about the rumours; Dorian shows him the corrupted portrait, and the night ends in murder.

174.label: The Opium Den
174.recap: Haunted and craving escape, Dorian visits an opium den, where Sibyl's vengeful brother nearly catches him.

174.label: The Portrait's Verdict
174.recap: Hoping to be good, Dorian turns a knife on the hideous portrait, and meets the fate it had borne for him.

215.label: Into the Primitive
215.recap: Buck, a pampered ranch dog, is stolen and sold north into the brutal world of sled dogs in the Klondike gold rush.

215.label: The Law of Club and Fang
215.recap: Buck learns the harsh law of club and fang and the savagery needed to survive among the sled teams.

215.label: The Dominant Primordial Beast
215.recap: The wild grows strong in Buck as he battles the cruel lead dog Spitz for mastery of the team.

215.label: The Toil of Trace and Trail
215.recap: Sold to greenhorns who cannot manage the trail, Buck and the worn team are driven to the edge of death.

215.label: For the Love of a Man
215.recap: Rescued by the kind John Thornton, Buck loves him fiercely and performs astonishing feats for his sake.

215.label: The Sounding of the Call
215.recap: With Thornton gone, nothing binds Buck to men any longer, and he answers the wild at last to run with the wolves.

768.label: Lockwood's Visit
768.recap: The new tenant Lockwood calls on his sullen landlord Heathcliff at the bleak, windswept Wuthering Heights.

768.label: A Ghost at the Window
768.recap: Snowbound overnight, Lockwood is terrified by a dream of a ghost child at the window begging to be let in.

768.label: The Foundling
768.recap: Housekeeper Nelly tells how old Earnshaw's adopted foundling Heathcliff and his daughter Catherine grew inseparable, to Hindley's jealousy.

768.label: Catherine and the Lintons
768.recap: Catherine returns from the refined Lintons changed, torn between wild Heathcliff and gentle Edgar Linton.

768.label: Heathcliff Vanishes
768.recap: Overhearing Catherine say it would degrade her to marry him, Heathcliff disappears into the storm.

768.label: His Return
768.recap: Years later, with Catherine now married to Edgar, a hardened and wealthy Heathcliff suddenly returns.

768.label: Catherine's Decline
768.recap: Caught between the two men, Catherine sinks into a feverish decline as Heathcliff schemes against the Lintons.

768.label: Catherine's Death
768.recap: Catherine dies giving birth to a daughter, leaving Heathcliff wild with grief and a desperate vow.

768.label: The Next Generation
768.recap: Years on, Catherine's daughter Cathy is drawn toward the Heights and Heathcliff's sickly son Linton.

768.label: Trapped at the Heights
768.recap: Heathcliff imprisons young Cathy and forces her to marry Linton so he can seize both family estates.

768.label: Lockwood Returns
768.recap: A year later Lockwood comes back to find young Cathy and the rough Hareton quietly drawn together.

768.label: Heathcliff's End
768.recap: Worn out by his obsession, Heathcliff stops eating and dies, longing only to rejoin his Catherine at last.

1342.label: A Single Man of Fortune
1342.recap: Mrs Bennet is delighted that wealthy Mr Bingley has taken nearby Netherfield, and at once schemes to match him with a daughter.

1342.label: The Meryton Assembly
1342.recap: At the local ball Bingley is taken with Jane, while his proud friend Mr Darcy slights Elizabeth, who overhears him.

1342.label: At Netherfield
1342.recap: Nursing an ill Jane at Netherfield, Elizabeth trades sharp words with the scornful Miss Bingley and the watchful Mr Darcy.

1342.label: Mr. Collins Arrives
1342.recap: The pompous clergyman Mr Collins, heir to Longbourn, arrives meaning to marry one of the Bennet girls.

1342.label: The Netherfield Ball
1342.recap: At the Netherfield ball Elizabeth dances with Darcy amid mutual sparring, while her family embarrasses itself.

1342.label: Collins Proposes
1342.recap: Mr Collins proposes to Elizabeth, who refuses him flatly, to her mother's despair.

1342.label: Charlotte's Choice
1342.recap: To Elizabeth's shock, her sensible friend Charlotte accepts Mr Collins for the comfort and security he offers.

1342.label: Darcy's Proposal
1342.recap: Darcy stuns Elizabeth with a proud declaration of love; insulted, she refuses him and throws his faults in his face.

1342.label: The Letter
1342.recap: Darcy's long letter explains his hand in parting Bingley from Jane and the real truth about Wickham, shaking Elizabeth's certainties.

1342.label: Pemberley
1342.recap: Touring Darcy's grand estate, Elizabeth hears him warmly praised and meets him again, unexpectedly gentle.

1342.label: Lydia's Flight
1342.recap: Word arrives that her reckless sister Lydia has run off with Wickham, threatening the whole family's name.

1342.label: Darcy Speaks Again
1342.recap: His feelings unchanged, Darcy proposes a second time, and now Elizabeth gladly accepts him.

1342.label: Two Weddings
1342.recap: With Jane and Elizabeth both happily married, the Bennet household settles at last into contentment.

1513.label: A Street Brawl
1513.recap: The feuding Montagues and Capulets clash in Verona until the Prince warns that the next to break the peace will die; lovesick Romeo pines for another.

1513.label: The Capulet Feast
1513.recap: At the Capulets' masked ball Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love at once, only to learn they are children of sworn enemies.

1513.label: The Balcony
1513.recap: Beneath Juliet's window the two pledge their love and resolve to marry in secret.

1513.label: Married in Secret
1513.recap: Friar Lawrence weds Romeo and Juliet, hoping their union might heal the families' feud.

1513.label: Mercutio and Tybalt
1513.recap: Tybalt kills Mercutio; a grief-stricken Romeo kills Tybalt in turn, and the Prince banishes Romeo from Verona.

1513.label: Parting at Dawn
1513.recap: Romeo flees to Mantua after their one night together, just as Juliet's father commands her to marry Paris.

1513.label: The Friar's Plan
1513.recap: Desperate, Juliet takes a potion from Friar Lawrence that will make her seem dead so she can slip away to Romeo.

1513.label: The Potion
1513.recap: Alone and afraid, Juliet drinks the potion and sinks into a deathlike sleep.

1513.label: The Tomb
1513.recap: Believing Juliet dead, Romeo drinks poison at her tomb; waking to find him gone, Juliet takes her own life, and the families at last make peace.

2554.label: The Plan
2554.recap: In stifling Petersburg the destitute ex-student Raskolnikov nurses a monstrous idea and slips out to rehearse it at a pawnbroker's.

2554.label: The Family's Burden
2554.recap: A letter reveals his sister Dounia means to marry a smug benefactor to save the family, sharpening Raskolnikov's torment.

2554.label: The Murder
2554.recap: Raskolnikov kills the old pawnbroker with an axe, and then her meek sister Lizaveta, who walks in on him.

2554.label: Fever and Dread
2554.recap: Wracked by fever, Raskolnikov hides the stolen goods and lurches between terror and reckless self-betrayal.

2554.label: Marmeladov's Death
2554.recap: He sees the drunkard Marmeladov crushed by a carriage and helps the grieving family, meeting the gentle Sonia.

2554.label: First Duel with Porfiry
2554.recap: Raskolnikov fences warily with the sly investigator Porfiry, who seems to read his every nerve.

2554.label: Sonia and Lazarus
2554.recap: Drawn to Sonia's faith and suffering, Raskolnikov has her read aloud the raising of Lazarus.

2554.label: The Second Duel
2554.recap: A second, crueler interview tightens Porfiry's psychological net around Raskolnikov.

2554.label: Confession to Sonia
2554.recap: Unable to bear it alone, Raskolnikov confesses the murders to Sonia, who begs him to give himself up.

2554.label: Porfiry's Visit
2554.recap: Porfiry comes to Raskolnikov, quietly reveals he knows the truth, and urges him to confess for his own soul.

2554.label: Svidrigailov's End
2554.recap: The corrupt Svidrigailov, his last schemes collapsed, drifts through a grim final night to its end.

2554.label: Giving Himself Up
2554.recap: Taking Sonia's cross, Raskolnikov walks to the police and confesses to the murders.

2554.label: Siberia
2554.recap: In a Siberian prison camp, with Sonia faithfully beside him, Raskolnikov begins at last to find his way toward renewal.

2641.label: The Bertolini
2641.recap: In a Florence pension Lucy and her fretful chaperon Charlotte are unhappy with their rooms, until the blunt Mr Emerson and his son offer to swap.

2641.label: In Santa Croce
2641.recap: Exploring a church without her guidebook, Lucy falls in with the kindly, unconventional Mr Emerson and his quiet son George.

2641.label: A Death in the Piazza
2641.recap: A sudden killing in the square sends Lucy fainting into George's arms, an encounter that quietly unsettles her.

2641.label: Among the Violets
2641.recap: On a country drive Lucy comes upon George on a hillside of violets, and he kisses her before Charlotte sweeps her away.

2641.label: They Return
2641.recap: Charlotte hurries Lucy out of Florence, and the two women bury the kiss in careful silence.

2641.label: Back at Windy Corner
2641.recap: Home in England, Lucy accepts a proposal from the polished, controlling Cecil Vyse.

2641.label: Cecil's Scheme
2641.recap: Amused by his country neighbours, Cecil mischievously arranges for two strangers to take a nearby villa.

2641.label: The Sacred Lake
2641.recap: George, it turns out, is the new neighbour; he joins Freddy and Mr Beebe for a wild, laughing bathe in the woodland pond.

2641.label: The Disaster Within
2641.recap: George kisses Lucy once more and declares his love; flustered, she orders him away as her engagement starts to feel wrong.

2641.label: Lying to George
2641.recap: Lucy sends George off for good, refusing to admit, even to herself, what she truly feels.

2641.label: Lying to Cecil
2641.recap: Seeing Cecil clearly at last, Lucy breaks off the engagement.

2641.label: Lying to Mr. Emerson
2641.recap: About to flee abroad to escape the muddle, Lucy meets old Mr Emerson, who gently makes her face the truth of her heart.

2641.label: A Room with a View
2641.recap: Lucy and George return to the Florence pension as newlyweds, the long muddle finally undone.

2701.label: Loomings
2701.recap: Restless and gloomy on land, Ishmael decides the cure is to go to sea, this time on a whaling voyage.

2701.label: The Spouter-Inn
2701.recap: At a crowded New Bedford inn Ishmael must share a bed with a fearsome, tattooed harpooneer, Queequeg.

2701.label: Bosom Friends
2701.recap: Ishmael and the noble cannibal Queequeg become fast friends and resolve to ship out together.

2701.label: The Pequod
2701.recap: In Nantucket the two sign aboard the Pequod, a weathered whaler whose captain, Ahab, stays strangely unseen.

2701.label: The Prophet
2701.recap: A ragged stranger, Elijah, darkly hints at the doom awaiting any crew that sails under Captain Ahab.

2701.label: Ahab
2701.recap: Days out to sea, Ahab at last appears on deck: grim, scarred, standing on a leg of carved whale ivory.

2701.label: The Quarter-Deck
2701.recap: Ahab nails a gold doubloon to the mast and binds the crew by oath to hunt one creature: the white whale, Moby Dick.

2701.label: The Whiteness of the Whale
2701.recap: Ishmael tells the legend of Moby Dick, the vast white whale that took Ahab's leg and now owns his soul.

2701.label: The First Lowering
2701.recap: At the first hunt Ahab unveils his own hidden boat-crew, and a squall nearly drowns Ishmael's whaleboat.

2701.label: The Castaway
2701.recap: The little cabin-boy Pip is left adrift in the sea; rescued at last, he is never the same, his wits set loose.

2701.label: The Try-Works
2701.recap: Rendering blubber by firelight at night, Ishmael is nearly hypnotized into steering the ship to ruin.

2701.label: Queequeg's Coffin
2701.recap: Gravely ill, Queequeg has a coffin built, then recovers; the box is later sealed into a spare life-buoy.

2701.label: The Chase, First Day
2701.recap: Moby Dick is sighted at last, and on the first day of the chase the white whale shatters Ahab's boat.

2701.label: The Chase, Second Day
2701.recap: The hunt rages on; the whale wrecks more boats and Ahab's shadowy harpooneer Fedallah is dragged under.

2701.label: The Chase, Third Day
2701.recap: On the third day Moby Dick staves in the Pequod itself; the ship goes down with all hands, Ahab among them.

5200.label: The Transformation
5200.recap: Gregor Samsa wakes transformed into a monstrous insect, and his family recoils as he fails to leave for work.

5200.label: A Prisoner at Home
5200.recap: Shut in his room, Gregor adjusts to his strange body while his sister warily feeds him and the household strains.

5200.label: The End
5200.recap: Wounded and unwanted, Gregor wastes away, and his relieved family turns hopefully toward the future.

64317.label: West Egg
64317.recap: Nick Carraway settles next to the mansion of the mysterious Gatsby and dines with his cousin Daisy and her brutish husband Tom.

64317.label: The Valley of Ashes
64317.recap: Tom drags Nick to the flat he keeps with his mistress Myrtle, where a drunken little party turns ugly.

64317.label: Gatsby's Party
64317.recap: At one of Gatsby's lavish parties Nick finally meets his host, a courteous young man wrapped in rumour.

64317.label: Gatsby's Past
64317.recap: Gatsby takes Nick to lunch and, through Jordan, reveals he loved Daisy years ago and longs to win her back.

64317.label: Reunion
64317.recap: Nick arranges tea, and Gatsby and Daisy meet again after five years, awkward at first and then radiant.

64317.label: The Truth About Gatsby
64317.recap: Gatsby's humble beginnings surface, Tom grows suspicious, and a party leaves Daisy cold.

64317.label: The Confrontation
64317.recap: On a sweltering day the rivalry erupts in a hotel room, and the drive home ends in a fatal accident.

64317.label: The Vigil
64317.recap: Gatsby keeps watch over Daisy through the night as the consequences of the accident close in around him.

64317.label: After
64317.recap: Nick arranges Gatsby's near-empty funeral and, sickened by the careless people he leaves behind, returns west.
